SETD5 Syndrome
Foundation
research · advocacy · community
About the Foundation
Built by Families.
Driven by Science.

We are a family-led nonprofit dedicated to advancing research for SETD5 Syndrome, a rare genetic condition, and building a global community of support, hope, and action. SETD5 Syndrome Foundation was built by parents who refused to wait — and we won't stop until every family has answers.

Research

Funding rigorous science from basic biology to candidate therapies — every grant chosen to move families forward.

Advocacy

Amplifying the SETD5 Syndrome community's voice to ensure faster diagnosis, better access, and informed care.

Community

Connecting 1,100+ families across six continents so no one navigates this diagnosis alone.

SETD5 Syndrome Foundation is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization. Donations are tax-deductible to the extent permitted by law.

INSIDE
Understanding the Condition
What Is SETD5
Syndrome?

SETD5 Syndrome is a rare genetic neurodevelopmental condition caused by a variant in the SETD5 gene, located on chromosome 3p25.3. SETD5 haploinsufficiency — one working copy of the gene instead of two — disrupts development in ways that can affect cognition, behavior, communication, and physical features.

Genetic Location

SETD5 gene · Chromosome 3p25.3 · Autosomal dominant · Histone methyltransferase function critical to brain development

How It Occurs Most cases arise as a de novo (spontaneous, new) mutation — not inherited from either parent. An estimated 10–20% of cases are inherited.
Diagnosis Chromosomal microarray (CMA) or whole-exome/genome sequencing (WES/WGS) — your genetics team will advise on the right approach.
First Described 2014 · Still growing as genetic testing expands globally
Also Known As
MRD23 ADID 23 SETD5-Related Disorder 3p25.3 Microdeletion Syndrome

"Every person with SETD5 Syndrome is unique. This is a condition of beautiful complexity, and our mission is to fully understand it and support those living with it."

— SETD5 Syndrome Foundation
Key Facts & Features
The Full Spectrum
of SETD5 Syndrome
1,100+ Families Worldwide
6 Continents Affected
2014 First Described
75+ Published Cases

Learning & Cognition

Intellectual disability, developmental delay, working memory difficulties, reading and writing challenges.

Speech & Language

Significant speech and language delays. Expressive language often more affected than comprehension. Some use AAC.

Behavior & Regulation

ADHD features, anxiety, sensory sensitivities, autism spectrum features, and preference for routine.

Neurological & Motor

Low muscle tone (hypotonia), motor delays, coordination difficulties, sleep disturbances, epilepsy in some individuals.

Social & Adaptive

Social communication challenges and reduced awareness of danger reported in some individuals.

Medical & Physical

Feeding difficulties in infancy, growth concerns, vision or hearing issues, congenital heart concerns in some.

Current Research Priorities
SETD5 Protein Function Global Patient Registry Drug Screening (Mouse Models) Natural History Study
Get Involved
You Don't Have
to Navigate
This Alone.

Whether you're newly diagnosed or have been part of the SETD5 Syndrome community for years, there's a place for you here.

1

Learn

Visit setd5syndrome.org for plain-language information about the genetics, symptoms, and daily life with SETD5 Syndrome, written for families first.

2

Connect

Join our Facebook family group at facebook.com/groups/setd5families to meet families navigating the same journey.

3

Stay Informed

Sign up for our newsletter at setd5syndrome.org for research updates, family resources, and foundation news.

4

Support the Research

Every dollar brings us closer to answers. Your gift funds the science that could change lives.

Make a Donation

Research doesn't wait. Neither do our families. A tax-deductible gift today keeps scientists at the bench.

setd5syndrome.org/donate
